scene.description: Citizen T-318. A witness statement and an evidence log that disagree — and each one rewrites the other while you read. The record is arguing with itself in real time.

fragments.label: The Witness Statement
fragments.text: 'I saw him leave before it happened.' You blink. Now it reads: 'I saw him arrive after.' The words changed while you held them.

fragments.label: The Evidence Log
fragments.text: The log contradicts the statement, then contradicts itself to match it, then breaks the match again. Neither side will hold still long enough to be wrong.

fragments.label: The Reviewing Keeper
fragments.text: The review field is filled in. By you. With two opposite verdicts, both timestamped now, both in your hand.

fragments.label: The Thing Both Agree On
fragments.symbol: In the flicker between edits, for one frame, both halves say the same sentence — then bury it. You almost caught it. It was a name.

interpretations.label: Freeze the record on the witness's version
Freeze it on the evidence version
Let it keep editing — the truth is the motion, not a frame
